summary: Sprint 8 changes are security-positive overall. All new endpoints correctly use get_current_user for authentication. Rate limiting has been applied consistently across new and modified endpoints. IDOR is properly prevented — all data queries filter by user_id. The analytics split preserves the existing security model. One concern: analytics_fx.py imports _get_cached_price (private function) and _cache_price (private function in analytics_metrics.py) from kis_price — using private APIs is a minor coupling risk. No hardcoded secrets, no injection vulnerabilities, no data exposure issues found.

findings.title: Importing private functions from kis_price module
findings.detail: analytics_fx.py imports _get_cached_price (prefixed with _ indicating private). analytics_metrics.py also imports _cache_price and _get_cached_price. Using private/internal functions creates tight coupling and the functions could change signatures without notice.
findings.fix: Expose get_cached_price and cache_price as public functions in kis_price.py or analytics_utils.py.

findings.title: fx-history endpoint does not validate currency_pair input
findings.detail: The currency_pair query parameter is passed directly to SQLAlchemy's WHERE clause via FxRateSnapshot.currency_pair == currency_pair. SQLAlchemy uses parameterized queries so SQL injection is not possible. However, an attacker can probe for any currency_pair value — there is no allowlist validation. This is a minor information leakage concern.
findings.fix: Add an allowlist: valid_pairs = {'USDKRW', 'EURKRW'}; if currency_pair not in valid_pairs: raise HTTPException(400, ...).
